To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix.

Let the matrix be:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

The characteristic polynomial is given by $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$ A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda \end{array}\right) $$

To simplify the calculation, let's factor out $\frac{1}{16}$ from the entire matrix. Note that:
$-\frac{27}{4} = -\frac{108}{16}$
$\frac{83}{8} = \frac{166}{16}$
$\frac{39}{8} = \frac{78}{16}$
$-\frac{43}{8} = -\frac{86}{16}$
$-\frac{41}{16} = -\frac{41}{16}$
$\frac{21}{16} = \frac{21}{16}$
$\frac{213}{16} = \frac{213}{16}$
$\frac{35}{8} = \frac{70}{16}$

So, $A = \frac{1}{16} \begin{pmatrix} 189
